D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Median Cut — Heckbert's Adaptive Palette (1982)

Repeatedly split the colour-space box along its longest axis at the median pixel-count, then take the mean of each box; gives a palette of any size adapted to the image distribution

Median-cut is GIF's canonical quantizer. Pros: adapts to the image content; better than uniform on portraits. Cons: balances sample count rather than perceptual error — can over-allocate to skin tones at the cost of saturated highlights. Modern alternatives: k-means, octree, neural quantizer.

Slide palette size k — palette adapts to the image colour histogram.
